title: Ballard Reports Q4 2024 Results
text: Ballard Power Systems reported challenging fourth-quarter results reflecting industry headwinds. Q4 revenue declined 48% year-over-year to $24.5 million, while full-year revenue fell 32% to $69.7 million. The company posted a net loss of $46.5 million in Q4 and $323.5 million for the full year. Gross margin in Q4 was -13%, improved 9 points year-over-year, while cash position reached $603.9 million, down from $751.1 million. Record order backlog totaled $173.5 million, up 41% from Q3, with a 12-month orderbook of $98.9 million. Fuel cell engines shipped exceeded 660 units, approximately 56 MW, up 30% year-over-year. Bus revenue grew 51% for the full year to $44.2 million, offsetting declines in truck, rail, and marine segments. CEO Randy MacEwen noted: 2024 was a tough year for the hydrogen and fuel cell industry due to policy uncertainty and project delays. However, he emphasized the company is winning in the market with strong order intake and customer wins. Total Operating Expenses guidance for 2025 is $100-120 million with capital expenditure of $15-25 million. No specific revenue or earnings guidance provided, with expected back-half weighted revenue distribution.
